translation_status.review_note: KO and VI translations were produced by an LLM (the agent on this branch) and have not been read by a native speaker. EN/RU were produced or revised by humans. All KO/VI translations pass automated script-consistency tests (no Cyrillic in KO/VI bodies, no Hangul in VI/RU bodies), which catch only the most egregious LLM artifacts — semantic accuracy and natural phrasing still need a human pass before paid promotion.

claims.claim: Anki added FSRS support in 23.10 (October 2023) and enabled it by default for new collections in 24.10+.
claims.note: Inline citation present in all 4 locales. Wording differentiates 23.10 introduction vs 24.10 default-on for new collections.

claims.claim: Anki desktop is free and open source; AnkiDroid free; AnkiWeb sync free; AnkiMobile iOS is the only paid piece.
Origa runs on Windows, Linux, macOS, Android, and web; iOS is planned but not shipping.
Origa OCR and STT models run locally (offline card creation).
Origa interface languages: English, Russian, Vietnamese, Korean.
WaniKani is radical-based, English interface, paid subscription.
Bunpro is grammar-focused SRS, web-based, English interface.
Duolingo is gamified and shallow for Japanese.
Migii is a JLPT test simulator (timed practice).

flags: KO and VI translations are machine_translated (LLM). Script-consistency tests guard against the most common LLM artifacts (cross-script hallucination), but native-speaker review is required before paid promotion.
gate_reason: All competitor claims carry inline external citations. FSRS wording verified against docs.ankiweb.net. RU article typo '食ьба' fixed. EN and RU are human-quality. KO and VI are LLM translations marked in `translation_status` — eligible to ship (script-consistency verified, content structure sound) but a human review pass is recommended before any paid promotion or rich-result eligibility push.

brand_voice_check.competitor_disparagement: none — every competitor has a 'when it is best' framing
